data.reasoningText: The user wants to make their code testable by replacing `DateTime.UtcNow` with a mockable abstraction. They're on .NET 10, which means `TimeProvider` (introduced in .NET 8) is available as the built-in abstraction. Let me look at their codebase first to understand the structure.

data.content: Now I can see your `ReportGenerator` class. Since you're on .NET 10, the built-in `TimeProvider` (available since .NET 8) is the right abstraction — no custom wrappers needed.
data.reasoningText:
The user is on .NET 10 and wants to mock time. Since .NET 8+, the built-in `TimeProvider` class is available — that's the right abstraction. I should modify the `ReportGenerator` to accept `TimeProvider` via constructor injection instead of using `DateTime.UtcNow` directly.

In tests, they can use `Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider` to control time deterministically.

Let me use the `generate-testability-wrappers` skill since this is exactly what it's designed for.

toolCalls.name: edit
